title: Dasystephana adscendens
taxonomy: Dasystephana adscendens is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae, which is commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. This plant belongs to the tribe Heliantheae. It is native to the Americas and is often found growing in dry, rocky areas. Dasystephana adscendens is a relatively small plant, typically growing to be around 10-30 centimeters in height. Further classification of Dasystephana adscendens may also include its genus and species names within the broader classification of plant taxonomy.

intro: Dasystephana adscendens, also known as the "Woolly Threadflower" or "Mexican Threadflower", is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family Asteraceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and promote wound healing. The bioactive constituents of Dasystephana adscendens include flavonoids, terpenes, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Dasystephana adscendens is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil to treat various health conditions, and is also used in some traditional remedies to treat fever, respiratory issues, and skin conditions.
